minimum_distinct_lineages_for_promotion: 2 with at least 1 positive comparator beyond opacity-only rows
minimum_authoritative_or_direct_source_f: 1 direct procurement/register/disclosure record plus 1 additional public-authority or document-grade comparator
current_local_status: not promotable yet; upgraded by first positive register comparator
why_not_promoted_now: Current package now includes one true positive public algorithm-register comparator, which strengthens distinctiveness, but a single Dutch exemplar without buyer-side procurement trace is still too thin for safe taxonomy promotion.
promotion_exclusion_rule: Do not promote if the positive comparator set is still a single-country singleton or if all positive rows remain register-style metadata without a second procurement/disclosure surface.
current_best_use: Reviewer-safe methods note, dashboard overlay, and disclosure-comparison appendix for public-sector disclosure asymmetry
next_smallest_publishability_move: Add one second non-Dutch procurement notice, contract page, or public algorithm-register row into the existing disclosure-comparison columns and re-test distinctiveness.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Disclosure-completeness ladder with register-positive versus buyer-side-trace-absent flag

minimum_authoritative_or_direct_source_f: 2 direct official, publisher, or platform authority records across at least 2 domains
current_local_status: not promotable yet; analytically strong but evidence-thin
why_not_promoted_now: Cross-domain recurrence is visible, but too many current rows are title-level or specialist-reporting-only, so a standalone category could overstate what the local package can currently prove.
promotion_exclusion_rule: Do not promote when cross-domain recurrence depends mainly on title-gloss multilingual leads or concept-only linkage to provenance standards.
current_best_use: Methods/theory note, attestation/provenance appendix, cluster-graph overlay
next_smallest_publishability_move: Capture one direct official impersonation/governance page and one direct publisher or institutional correction/retraction notice so the pattern is not carried mainly by weak leads.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Provenance-confusion cluster graph spanning synthetic-media and document-chain failures

minimum_authoritative_or_direct_source_f: 2 authoritative rows, with at least 1 settled or regulator-final hidden-human/autonomy-theater exemplar
current_local_status: not promotable yet; one authoritative charging-stage anchor only
why_not_promoted_now: The Nate row proves the proposal is no longer purely conceptual, but the strongest final-authority support still concerns generic AI-washing/capability misstatement rather than concealed human labor itself.
promotion_exclusion_rule: Do not promote if hidden-human support is accusation-stage only or if the second example is merely generic hype without explicit human-labor concealment relevance.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Find a judgment, order, consent decree, or later public disposition that explicitly documents hidden human processing behind marketed AI capability claims.

minimum_authoritative_or_direct_source_f: 1 authoritative or institutional record with an explicit blocked challenge, appeal, or reconstruction path
current_local_status: not promotable yet; concept visible, challenge-path evidence weak
why_not_promoted_now: Current rows hint at failed reviewability, but the package rarely exposes the actual appeal, contestation, or formal redress mechanism in enough detail to sustain a distinct category claim.
promotion_exclusion_rule: Do not promote if the anomaly is only surprising or harmful without a documented missing or failed challenge/reconstruction pathway.
current_best_use: Law-to-evidence limitation note and thesis queue
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prioritize one court complaint, decree, institutional correction notice, or formal appeal record where the blocked challenge path is explicit and not inferred.

minimum_authoritative_or_direct_source_f: 2 public cases with documented delegated permissions, approval scope, or institutional authority design
current_local_status: not promotable yet; governance pattern remains mostly interpretive
why_not_promoted_now: The security rows strongly show tool/control failures, but they rarely document formal authority allocation, delegated approval scope, or institutional oversight design cleanly enough for a new category.
promotion_exclusion_rule: Do not promote when the supporting rows expose only technical exploitability or unsafe capability without explicit evidence about authority delegation or governance scope.
current_best_use: Thesis hypothesis and security-governance bridge memo
next_smallest_publishability_move: Wait for public cases with documented permissions, delegated powers, or institutional authority grants beyond generic CVE/advisory framing.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Permission-scope topology and authority-overhang caution panel
